steps.title: Identify the causal relationship
steps.content: The key signal is the flow from modern planes having sleeker, more streamlined bodies directly to much more aerodynamic. This establishes that the improved aerodynamics is a consequence of the streamlined design.

steps.title: Select the causal transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect — modern planes have sleeker, more streamlined bodies (cause), so they are much more aerodynamic (effect). The blank must signal that result.
  • Choice A — "However,": signals contrast or unexpected qualification; the aerodynamic improvement is not a surprising counterpoint to the streamlined bodies — it is the direct result. ✗
  • Choice B — "Nevertheless,": signals that something is true despite an obstacle; there is no obstacle here — streamlined design straightforwardly produces better aerodynamics. ✗
  • Choice C — "Lastly,": signals the final item in an enumerated list; no list is being presented, and aerodynamics is not a listed characteristic but a causal outcome. ✗
  • Choice D — "For this reason,": signals that what follows is a direct consequence of what just preceded it — the streamlined bodies are why modern planes are more aerodynamic. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
